title: Prestwick Airport: John Swinney seeking UK Government meeting over US military use of airport

sections.heading: Meeting request
sections.content: John Swinney is seeking a meeting with the Cabinet Office after US military aircraft stopped at Prestwick Airport while travelling to the Middle East. The Scotsman said flight logs indicated aircraft directly involved in supporting US operations had transited the Scottish Government-owned airport in recent weeks.

sections.heading: Flights highlighted
sections.content: According to the report, three Boeing KC-135 Stratotankers left Riverside Air Force Base in California on 17 and 18 February, stopped at Prestwick, and then flew on to Saudi Arabia between 19 and 22 February before deploying in Israel. One of those aircraft was later recorded flying east towards Iran on 28 February, and by early March all three appeared to be based at Ben Gurion Airport near Tel Aviv.

sections.heading: Official responses
sections.content: The Scottish Greens said Prestwick was acting as a staging post for military activity linked to the conflict and urged ministers to be transparent about what they knew. The Scottish Government said non-UK military flights using UK airspace require Ministry of Defence permission and that it had asked the UK Government to confirm that no aircraft had passed through Scottish Government-owned airports as part of offensive operations against Iran. The MoD said Prestwick was a civilian airport and was not being used by the US to launch strikes.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Prestwick Airport: Swinney seeks UK Government meeting on US military use of airport
